creator: crest@rlwinm.de
text: The MBR limitations have been overcome with GPT. Most operating systems have switched to GPT and UEFI booting. The in-kernel ext2/3 support is limited to small inodes and lacks journaling support. For full ext3/4 support you have to use fuse which has its own share of problems and limitations. The best file system to share data between FreeBSD and Linux at the moment is ZFS. In theory you could even install FreeBSD and Linux into the same ZFS pool.
